heading: Acquisition of lands
label: 2406 Acquisition of lands

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: As additional lands are needed for national cemeteries, they may be acquired by the Secretary by purchase, gift (including donations from States or political subdivisions thereof), condemnation, transfer from other Federal agencies, exchange, or otherwise, as the Secretary determines to be in the best interest of the United States.

children.heading: .—Transfer of Department of the Interior Land for Use as a National Cemetery
children.label: b .—Transfer of Department of the Interior Land for Use as a National Cemetery

children.text: withdrawn from all forms of appropriation under the public land laws, including the mining laws, the mineral leasing laws, and the geothermal leasing laws;
transferred to the administrative jurisdiction of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs; and
deemed to be property (as defined in ) for as long as the land remains under the administrative jurisdiction of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.section 102(9) of title 40
